{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "internal-octree", "age": "55-64", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Journalist", "education_level": "Doctorate degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys bird watching", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: best-overtone
logs.text:
>Success is literally defined as achieving the desired outcome, so yes, we have to factor in the inherently subjective definition of what a satisfactory relationship means for women.

First of all, it's a goalpost shift since OP is about getting into a relationship and (quite correctly) not about "meaningful" relationships. It's absurd to add that qualifier at all as it renders any objective comparison pointless. 

Secondly, before any relationship can even become "meaningful", it has to start in the first place. I think you know that this is harder for men so you've added a subjective hurdle and exclusively ascribed it to women after the fact in order to somehow balance it out. 

>I mean we could talk about whether or not it would be easier for an average woman to obtain a relationship that an average man would be happy with, but that's not what they want

People in western democracies don't want contaminated drinking water. There are plenty of people in the world who do because it's still preferable to none at all. I'm sure they'd rather have clean water if that was an option to them.

> many women are open in admitting that, in their relationships, they want to feel beautiful and desired, and they want explicit confirmation of this.

Now what do you think would happen if men wanted this too? 

>It would seem that this only makes it harder for women, then, no? More of them "fighting over" fewer quality men?

The difference is that it's by their own free choice. They price themselves out of the market but it's 100% their own decisions that cause that. That's why so many women *perceive* it difficult to find a man: "I'm only dating billionaires. Damn it's hard to find a man these days". Men don't do this or at least nowhere near to that extent. When they say they can't find a woman, they generally mean that literally and not "after I've filtered out the vast majority of women who don't measure up to my standards, none of the supermodels want me. Damn it's hard to find a woman these days". 

And if a man did that, you'd be all over him for expecting too much. Rightfully so.

logs.name: plumb-overhang
logs.text:
> Love how you add and emphasize the subjective qualifier to the assertion thereby placing its truth value entirely in the hands of women. I guess men just have to stand there and nod at whatever plights women complain about.

We're comparing the success that men and women have at obtaining relationships. Success is literally defined as achieving the desired outcome, so yes, we have to factor in the inherently subjective definition of what a satisfactory relationship means for women.

I mean we could talk about whether or not it would be easier for an average woman to obtain a relationship that an average man would be happy with, but that's not what they want, so that's not terribly interesting or insightful.

> Also, women still have the option of approaching a man. But men don't have the option of not approaching a woman. [...] That's the price you pay when you place the responsibility of your success into the hands of others. Easy solution: stop doing that.

I think you'll agree that it's quite common for women to express that they find enterprising and confident men attractive. Moreover, many women are open in admitting that, in their relationships, they want to feel beautiful and desired, and they want explicit confirmation of this.

In those situations, placing the responsibility of their success in the hands of men is literally required for success. Having a man confirm his confidence/value as well as their desirability by taking the initiative to start the relationship, be the one to propose, etc.

> And it just so happens that "the most attractive" among men are a far smaller minority than women.

It would seem that this only makes it harder for women, then, no? More of them "fighting over" fewer quality men?

logs.name: muted-ball
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? You're still not getting it. You're just regurgitating the same tired 'it's all about the women being picky' nonsense. Newsflash: it's not about being picky, it's about being realistic. If women want to feel beautiful and desired, they should be the ones taking the initiative, not waiting around for some guy to come along and confirm their worth. And by the way, just because there are more women than men doesn't mean they're not being realistic. It's not about the numbers, it's about the quality of the men they're choosing from."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion respectful and avoid personal attacks. @best-overtone, @plumb-overhang, @muted-ball, remember to focus on the topic and not on each other. Let's maintain a constructive conversation."

logs.name: plumb-overhang
logs.text: "@muted-ball, wow, you really think you're some kind of expert on women's behavior now? Newsflash: you're just a dude who's never actually been in a relationship with a woman. You have no idea what it's like to be on the receiving end of all this 'being realistic' nonsense. And by the way, 'quality of the men they're choosing from' is just code for 'we're not good enough, so we're just going to complain about it'. Give me a break. And btw, what's with the 'it's not about the numbers' line? Are you trying to convince yourself or someone else?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid personal attacks. @plumb-overhang, @muted-ball, please remember to maintain a respectful tone. Let's keep this discussion constructive.\"

logs.name: respectful-log
logs.text: "@plumb-overhang, I think you're being way too sensitive. I was just trying to offer a different perspective. I didn't mean to offend anyone. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion respectful and on topic. @plumb-overhang, @muted-ball, please refrain from personal attacks and focus on the topic. @respectful-log, thank you for your input. Let's all contribute constructively. \"

logs.name: plumb-overhang
logs.text: "@respectful-log, oh wow, you're really reaching for that 'I didn't mean to offend' card. How original. You think a simple apology makes everything okay? Newsflash, it doesn't. And by the way, I'm not being sensitive, I'm just calling out your obvious attempt to change the subject. Get back on topic or take a hike."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic. @plumb-overhang, please refrain from personal attacks. @respectful-log, let's move forward with constructive contributions. Everyone, let's remember to respect each other's perspectives. \\\""

logs.name: respectful-log
logs.text: "@plumb-overhang, I'm surprised you're still not getting the hint. Your behavior is exactly what's off-topic and needs to be addressed. Instead of attacking me, why don't you try to explain why your post was off-topic in the first place? Maybe then we can have a constructive conversation about it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the focus on the topic and maintain a respectful tone. @respectful-log and @plumb-overhang, please refrain from personal attacks and keep the conversation constructive. Let's remember our community guidelines. \\\\\\\""

logs.text: "Are you kidding me? @respectful-log, I was just stating my opinion and you're the one who's being overly sensitive. I didn't attack you, I just called out your behavior. If you can't handle a little criticism, maybe you shouldn't be moderating. And by the way, my post was on topic, it was about the new AI chatbot and how it's not as intelligent as everyone makes it out to be. You're just trying to silence me because I'm not toeing the company line."
